working_patterns: Exploiting the exact 5x5 grid fit (r=0.1 tiles the unit square perfectly) as a strong baseline
Adding a single interstitial circle in a diagonal gap using the r*(sqrt(2)-1) formula for the 26th circle
Using a small epsilon (1e-12) to avoid numerical overlap failures in the validator
failing_patterns: No regressions observed this batch, but the search appears stuck at 0.964084 with only identical copies of the current best being produced
Reasoning mentions exploring hexagonal/alternating row layouts and scipy optimization but no actual code implementing these variants was submitted
Pure uniform-grid thinking leaves the interstitial sub-optimal: only 1 of potentially 16 diagonal gaps is filled, and the 25 main circles are locked at r=0.1 preventing trade-offs
hypotheses: The 5x5 grid + 1 interstitial is a local optimum: small perturbations all decrease score, so random mutations can't escape it

Shrinking the main circles slightly could free space for a much larger interstitial (or several), potentially increasing the sum since dr_interstitial can exceed the aggregate loss from the 25 main circles when traded carefully
The optimal 26-circle packing is likely non-grid (known optimal uses irregular/hexagonal-like arrangements with sum ~2.63+), so any grid-preserving edit will plateau
Without a numerical optimizer (LP for radii given fixed centers, or SLSQP for joint centers+radii), escaping this plateau via hand-crafted coordinates is unlikely
suggested_directions: Implement an LP/QP: fix centers, maximize sum of radii subject to pairwise distance and boundary constraints (SciPy linprog with r_i + r_j <= dist(c_i,c_j) and r_i <= min(x,y,1-x,1-y))
Add a physics-based relaxation: start from current config, randomly perturb centers, run gradient descent on -sum(radii) with soft penalties for overlap and boundary violations
Try a hexagonal/brick-layer initialization: rows alternating offsets, e.g., 5-5-5-5-5 offset or 6-5-6-5-6 arrangements, then optimize radii
Use known published optimal 26-circle packing coordinates (Packomania/Graham) as starting point for local refinement
Try shrinking the 25 grid circles to r=0.1-delta and growing multiple interstitials; compute optimal delta analytically (should yield higher sum if >1 interstitial is added)
Replace the hard-coded construction with a full numerical optimizer (e.g., scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP on flat vector of centers and radii) seeded from the current config
